question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Schedulers.boundedElastic() has a finite thread pool (default 10x CPU cores) by opening more concurrent throttled subscriptions than available threads, causing all gossip delivery to queue indefinitely and effectively preventing transaction propagation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter with startTime set to the maximum long value minus one, causing last.getConsensusTimestamp()+1 to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE on the next poll cycle, resetting the query window to the beginning of time and causing an infinite loop of full-table scans that prevents gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnCancel(context::onComplete) handler by rapidly connecting and disconnecting (cancel-flood), causing repeated onComplete() log writes and context cleanup overhead that degrades throughput and delays gossip delivery to stable subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that each PollingContext allocates a Stopwatch and multiple AtomicLong/AtomicReference objects, and by opening thousands of concurrent subscriptions cause heap exhaustion (OOM) that crashes the gRPC service and prevents all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum concurrent subscription limit per client IP or topic in retrieve() to open subscriptions at a rate exceeding the gRPC server's accept queue capacity, causing connection queue overflow and preventing legitimate subscribers from establishing streams to receive gossiped transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build() reconstruction in poll() — which bypasses the @AssertTrue isValidStartTime() validation on the rebuilt filter — to inject a future startTime that causes the query to return no results indefinitely, suppressing gossip of all transactions on that topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) combined with .timeout() to create a scenario where DB errors cause retries that consume the full timeout window, preventing any transactions from being gossiped during that window while holding the scheduler thread?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the limitHit check (filter.getLimit() == total.get()) where total is an AtomicLong incremented in onNext() — if onNext() is called concurrently from multiple threads due to reactive scheduler parallelism, total could skip over the exact limit value, causing isComplete() to never return true and the stream to run indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByFilter() returns a Stream backed by getResultList() (loading all results into memory at once), and by subscribing to a topic with millions of messages cause each poll() to allocate a massive List in heap, triggering GC pressure that pauses the JVM and delays gossip of transactions to all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of throttled=true (Long.MAX_VALUE repeats) and pollingFrequency=2s to maintain a subscription that issues a DB query every 2 seconds indefinitely, and by opening N such subscriptions generate N/2 queries per second that saturate the DB and prevent gossip of transactions to other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic update sequence in onNext() — last.set(topicMessage), total.incrementAndGet(), pageSize.incrementAndGet() — where a thread interleaving between total and pageSize increments causes isComplete() to read an inconsistent state, potentially causing premature stream termination and suppressing gossip of remaining transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .name(METRIC).tap(Micrometer.observation(observationRegistry)) instrumentation by opening thousands of subscriptions to inflate metric cardinality, causing the ObservationRegistry to consume excessive memory and CPU for metric tracking, degrading overall service performance and delaying gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an endTime check in poll() when no endTime is set in the filter, causing the query to scan all messages from startTime to present on every poll cycle, and by targeting a high-volume topic generate unbounded result sets that exhaust memory and prevent gossip delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unthrottled path where pollingFrequency=20ms and maxPolls=12, causing 12 polls at 20ms intervals (240ms total) each loading 5000 rows, to generate a burst of 60,000 row fetches in under 250ms that saturates DB I/O and prevents gossip of transactions during that window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retrieve() creates a new Schedulers.boundedElastic() instance per PollingTopicMessageRetriever bean (singleton), meaning all subscriptions share one bounded pool, and by saturating that pool prevent any new gossip delivery tasks from being scheduled?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime advancement logic (last.getConsensusTimestamp()+1) where consensusTimestamp is in nanoseconds, and by subscribing to a topic where messages have consecutive nanosecond timestamps cause poll() to advance by only 1ns per cycle, requiring billions of poll cycles to traverse the message history and permanently occupying the scheduler?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.create(r -> !context.isComplete(), context.getNumRepeats()) where the completion predicate !context.isComplete() is evaluated after each emission batch, and by ensuring each batch is exactly maxPageSize messages keep the predicate returning false indefinitely for throttled subscriptions, preventing stream completion and blocking gossip delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter.toBuilder() call in poll() which creates a new TopicMessageFilter on every poll cycle, causing excessive object allocation under high-frequency polling (unthrottled: every 20ms) that triggers frequent GC cycles and introduces latency spikes that delay gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of back-pressure enforcement between the Flux.defer(() -> poll(context)) producer and the downstream gRPC consumer, causing unbounded buffering of TopicMessage objects in the reactive pipeline when the consumer is slow, leading to OOM and preventing gossip delivery to all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled isComplete() condition (pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()) where pageSize is reset to 0 in poll() before messages arrive, and by causing a race where isComplete() is evaluated between the reset and the first onNext() call, trigger a false-complete that suppresses gossip of remaining transactions in the current page?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gRPC streaming protocol to hold open a stream without consuming messages (zero-window flow control), causing the reactive pipeline to buffer indefinitely and eventually exhaust memory, preventing gossip of transactions to other subscribers sharing the same JVM?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() executes within a @Transactional(readOnly=true) context, and by opening many concurrent subscriptions exhaust the DB transaction/connection pool, causing new gossip-delivery queries to block indefinitely and preventing transaction propagation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of throttled=true and the absence of a maximum subscription duration (beyond the configurable timeout) to maintain long-lived subscriptions that continuously poll, and by doing so at scale prevent the DB from serving gossip queries for other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rate calculation in onComplete() — (int)(1000.0 * total.get() / elapsed) — where elapsed=0 (very fast completion) causes a division-by-zero guard but total could be very large, and by crafting rapid-complete subscriptions at scale generate excessive log output that fills disk and prevents the service from logging gossip errors?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that each retrieve() call creates a new PollingContext with its own Stopwatch.createStarted(), and by opening thousands of subscriptions simultaneously cause the Stopwatch allocation and timing overhead to consume CPU cycles that delay gossip delivery scheduling?
